- randomize channel/video switching and start offset
- hide player controls for TV-like playback
- add screenshots to README and ignore runtime files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: resolve all PSScriptAnalyzer lint violations

Auto-fixed formatting across all 55 PowerShell files:
- Brace placement (PSPlaceOpenBrace, PSPlaceCloseBrace)
- Indentation consistency (PSUseConsistentIndentation)
- Whitespace around operators and braces (PSUseConsistentWhitespace)

Manually fixed blocking errors:
- Remove unused variables: logmanOut, lastSuccessfulBranch,
  lastImproved, skipExperiment, applySuccess, config, deltaRPS, sTotalWidth
- Replace empty catch blocks with Write-Verbose in Invoke-CopilotAgent.ps1,
  HoneHelpers.psm1, and Show-Progress.ps1
- Use named parameters for Get-CounterStats calls
- Add OutputType attribute to Start-Spinner
- Suppress unused interface-contract parameters with SuppressMessage
- Remove trailing whitespace in config.psd1

Result: 55 files, 0 errors, 0 warnings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

messages: fix three regression bugs introduced by global manager thread refactoring (#560)

* Initial plan

* feat: update messages module for global manager visibility with building→unit compose flow

- Add _msgGetBuildingById/_msgFindUnit/_msgFormatUnitLabel/_msgGetManagedBuildings helpers
- Replace raw unitCode display with formatted 'Stan br. X • Street Y' label in thread list and detail
- Manager thread list now aggregates threads from ALL managed buildings (global view)
- Compose form: two-level building→unit selection; unit dropdown populated dynamically after building selection; uses DOM methods (XSS-safe)
- validateNewThreadForm() now requires buildingId as first param
- Thread submit uses selected building ID, not activeBid()
- Per-thread operations (fetch msgs, mark-read, reply) use thread.bid
- Tenant view unchanged; tenants see only own unit threads from active building
- Badge update handles null activeBid for managers (falls back to first managed building)

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: restore dropped var declarations in renderMessageThreadDetail and renderMessageThreadList

renderMessageThreadDetail() and renderMessageThreadList() both had 4–5 `var` declarations
accidentally omitted when the previous edit replaced the safeUnit line. The template strings
still referenced safeKind, safeStatus, safeDate (and isSelected in the list function),
which would cause ReferenceError at runtime on every thread render.

Restored: safeKind, safeStatus, ts, safeDate in renderMessageThreadDetail();
          safeKind, safeStatus, ts, safeDate, isSelected in renderMessageThreadList().
safeUnit now correctly uses _msgFormatUnitLabel() in both functions.

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: restore tenant reply capability — canReply = isManage || isTenant

var canReply = isManage at line 761 was introduced by the compose-form refactoring
and silently disabled reply for tenant (stanar) users. All underlying reply machinery
(saveReplyToThread, _msgFetchMessages, _msgBindReplyForm) works correctly for any role;
only the canReply gate was wrong.

The compose panel and compose form binding remain gated on isManage only, so tenants
still cannot create new threads — they can only reply to existing ones.

Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: bgnikolapopovic-cloud <252382813+bgnikolapopovic-cloud@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: use ProcessStartInfo.ArgumentList for proper argument quoting

Start-Process -ArgumentList joins array elements with spaces without
quoting. The copilot prompt argument contains spaces, newlines, and
special characters that got split into multiple arguments, completely
breaking copilot CLI invocations.

Replaced Start-Process with System.Diagnostics.Process using
ProcessStartInfo.ArgumentList (a .NET Collection<string>) which
properly quotes each argument per OS conventions. Also reads stdout
asynchronously to prevent deadlocks from buffer fill.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Unify trace ID: use OTel trace ID when available, custom ID as fallback

When OTel is active, the traceId in logs matches the OTel trace ID,
enabling direct correlation between Pino logs in Loki and traces in
Tempo. When OTel is off, falls back to the sequential request ID
(e.g., '042x00015') for local debugging.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: address 7 bugs found during audit

- Invoke-CopilotAgent.ps1: move \\\ before try block;
  restore encoding on timeout return path
- HoneHelpers.psm1: rewrite Wait-ApiHealthy with Stopwatch for
  accurate timing; check health immediately before sleeping;
  clamp sleep to remaining time
- Start-SampleApi.ps1: remove undefined \\\ in log message;
  add Stop-Process cleanup before port retry
- Write-HoneLog.ps1: use script-scoped cache for MaxFileSizeMB
  so log rotation works when \\\ is provided directly
- Invoke-HoneLoop.ps1: add -BaseBranch to 6 early
  Add-ExperimentMetadata calls that defaulted to master
- Invoke-AllScaleTests.ps1: add -SkipHealthCheck param and
  pass through to Invoke-ScaleTests.ps1 calls; pass from loop

commit.message:
Implement all harness-improvements.md recommendations (Tiers 1-7)

Tier 1 - Reliability & Safety:
- Add Copilot CLI timeout (Invoke-CopilotWithTimeout, AgentTimeoutSec config)
- Add k6 diagnostic timeout (K6TimeoutSec config)
- Fix SQL injection in Reset-Database.ps1 (escape ] in db name)
- Add path traversal guard in Apply-Suggestion.ps1
- Cap Get-PctChange return value to prevent floating-point infinity

Tier 2 - Code Duplication & Shared Abstractions:
- Create HoneHelpers.psm1 shared module (Write-Status, Get-HoneConfig,
  Wait-ApiHealthy, Limit-String + extracted loop helpers)
- Create Invoke-CopilotAgent.ps1 unified agent runner (model resolution,
  UTF-8 encoding, spinner, timeout, JSON extraction, retry)
- Create Stage-ExperimentArtifacts.ps1 (replaces 3 identical staging blocks)
- Unify health check retry logic via Wait-ApiHealthy
- Remove Write-Status duplication from 10 files
- Replace Import-PowerShellDataFile boilerplate in 24 files with Get-HoneConfig

Tier 3 - Main Loop Decomposition:
- Create Invoke-FailureHandler.ps1 (unified rejection sequence)
- Create Build-PRBody.ps1 (accepted + rejected PR body construction)
- Extract Add-ExperimentMetadata, New-ExperimentPR, Build-StackNote,
  Undo-ExperimentBranch to HoneHelpers.psm1
- Remove legacy analysis response format support

Tier 4 - Robustness & Resource Lifecycle:
- Add try/finally guards for process cleanup in diagnostic and scale test scripts
- Atomic queue writes in Manage-OptimizationQueue.ps1 (temp file + rename)
- Validate dotnet-counters attachment after startup
- Add log rotation in Write-HoneLog.ps1 (configurable MaxFileSizeMB)
- Add port reuse retry loop in Start-SampleApi.ps1

Tier 5 - Testability:
- Extract Compare-Metrics pure function from Compare-Results.ps1
- Add DryRun mock agent support (MockResponsePath param + test fixtures)
- Create docs/plugin-contracts.md with collector/analyzer interface specs

Tier 6 - Configuration & Validation:
- Create Test-HoneConfig.ps1 (validates tolerances, paths, ports, tools)
- Add per-metric absolute thresholds (MinAbsoluteRPSDelta, MinAbsoluteErrorRateDelta)
- Document config interactions in docs/configuration.md

Tier 7 - Performance & Minor:
- Remove legacy analysis format support (~60 lines)
- Add -SkipHealthCheck to Invoke-ScaleTests.ps1
- Cache log path in Write-HoneLog.ps1

36 files changed, 1857 insertions(+), 1297 deletions(-)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Apply suggestions from code review

Co-authored-by: Copilot Autofix powered by AI <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Create .PSScriptAnalyzerSettings.psd1 with sensible defaults for the harness

commit.message:
Support tiktok videos broken (#176)

* Initial plan

* Add TikTok platform-aware support across recorder, editor and runtime sync

Co-authored-by: animyrch <42338841+animyrch@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix TikTok recording flow: accept TikTok URLs in /react, embed TikTok iframe in recorder

Co-authored-by: animyrch <42338841+animyrch@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix TikTok recorder: player/v1 API, vertical aspect ratio, postMessage play/pause

Co-authored-by: animyrch <42338841+animyrch@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix TikTok display in reaction/edit pages and backend player height

Co-authored-by: animyrch <42338841+animyrch@users.noreply.github.com>

* Refactor twin players: remove unused UI update function and enhance YouTube API mock for testing

* Add TikTok original sync fixture and enhance player mock for testing

* feat: enhance original video metadata handling for TikTok and YouTube

- Added support for fetching and normalizing original video metadata from TikTok and YouTube.
- Updated `CreatorDetails` component to display original video author URL and platform.
- Modified `useTwinPlayers` to include original video metadata in state management.
- Implemented new helper functions for building URLs and fetching metadata.
- Enhanced reaction document creation to include original video details.
- Updated shared session management to handle original video platform and URL.
- Refactored API endpoint for fetching original video metadata.
- Adjusted various routes to utilize the new metadata fetching logic.

* Add playlist sequence support and recorder state

Introduce canonical playlist sequencing and recorder-state utilities, plus related UI, backend and API updates.

Highlights:
- Add src/lib/helpers/reactionSequence.js: canonical sequenceItems schema, parsing, normalization, conversion helpers and playlist assignment logic to support duplicate-safe mixed YouTube/TikTok playlists while remaining backward-compatible with originalVideoIds.
- Add src/lib/helpers/recorderState.js: recorder player state constants and helper to build recorder state configs for storing reaction/original time+state mappings.
- Update Firebase helpers to create/update playlists using sequenceItems (createPlaylistDocument, addToPlaylistDocument, getUserPlaylists) and to use new reactionSequence helpers.
- Update UI components: PlaylistQueue and PlaylistQueueItem now consume sequenceItems, support hiding the current item, stable keys (id), and build canonical navigation params (platform/sequenceIndex/originalUrl). Also use buildYouTubeThumbnailUrl helper.
- Extend useTwinPlayers composable to handle sequenceItems: find current index, populate playlistItems from sequence, preserve reaction timing across consecutive identical originals, reuse/destroy original players correctly, restore iframe containers, and apply richer original metadata.
- Add originalVideo helper buildYouTubeThumbnailUrl and enhance youtube helpers with playlist preview/pagination/fetchAll functions and updated server endpoint to return next-page token and optional playlist title.
- Update backend recorder page to wire sequenceIndex, load sequenceItems for recorder flows, use recorder state helpers to persist reaction state, and adapt TikTok state codes.
- Docs updated (SCHEMA_REFERENCE.md) to document sequenceItems schema and version bump; integration tests added for reaction-sequence and recorder-state.

This change enables reliable ordered playback/recording of playlists that may contain duplicates or mixed platforms while maintaining legacy compatibility.

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(stdlib): add std::encoding::xml for XML parsing and serialization (#209)

* feat(stdlib): add std::encoding::xml for XML parsing and serialization

Implement the std::encoding::xml module wrapping the quick-xml crate
via Rust FFI, following the same pattern as std::encoding::json.

API:
- xml.parse(s) — parse XML string into a Node tree
- node.to_string() — serialize back to XML
- node.get_attribute(name) — get attribute value (empty if missing)
- node.get_child(index) — get child node by index
- node.get_text() — concatenated text content (recursive)
- node.get_tag() — element tag name
- node.children_count() — number of child nodes
- node.is_element() — element vs text node check
- node.free() — release node resources

Includes:
- Rust staticlib with 9 unit tests
- E2E test parsing a document with attributes, children, and text
- Registered in workspace Cargo.toml, Makefile, and CMakeLists.txt

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(stdlib): add std/math module with integer and float operations (#212)

Add a new std::math stdlib module providing:

Integer functions: abs, min, max, clamp, sign
Float functions: abs_f, sqrt, pow, floor, ceil, round
Constants: pi(), e()

The codegen backend maps math.* calls to MLIR math-dialect intrinsics
(SqrtOp, PowFOp, FloorOp, CeilOp, RoundOp, etc.) for optimal
performance. Integer operations (clamp, sign) use arith dialect ops
(MinSIOp, MaxSIOp, CmpIOp, SelectOp).

The Rust FFI crate (hew-std-math) provides fallback implementations
wrapping f64 methods from libm.

Includes E2E test covering all functions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(stdlib): add std::net::tls for TLS client connections (#205)

* feat(stdlib): add std::net::tls for TLS client connections

Implement TLS client module wrapping rustls with ring crypto provider,
following the same pattern as std::net::quic. Provides:

- connect(host, port) -> TlsStream — open TLS connection with webpki roots
- write(stream, data) -> int — write bytes to stream
- read(stream, size) -> bytes — read bytes from stream
- close(stream) — close connection and free resources

Includes Rust unit tests for null-safety on all FFI entry points.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix(stdlib): add unsafe wrappers and fix type coercions for type-check compliance (#211)

Wrap all extern FFI calls in `unsafe { }` blocks across 31 stdlib
modules. Add explicit `as i32` casts where the type checker now rejects
implicit i64-to-i32 coercions (base64, csv, hex, jwt, path, semver,
string). Rewrite string.is_ascii to use char comparison instead of
unsupported char-to-int casts. Add stub return values in builtins.hew
so empty-bodied builtin declarations pass type-checking.

Update stdlib_loader to look through Expr::Unsafe nodes when extracting
FFI call targets from impl method bodies (required for handle-method
discovery after the unsafe wrapping).

All 42 std/*.hew files now pass `hew check`. All 459 codegen E2E tests
and 307 Rust unit tests continue to pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: hew
repository.description: A statically-typed, actor-oriented programming language for concurrent and distributed systems.

commit.message:
Retry next queue item when architecture is skipped

When a queue item is classified as architecture, the loop now tries
the next pending item instead of wasting the entire experiment. This
prevents the exp 18 scenario where the only picked item was skipped
and the experiment produced no code change or PR.

The GetNext  classify  architecture-check is now wrapped in a while
loop. Architecture items are still marked done/skipped and logged.
The experiment only skips if ALL remaining queue items are exhausted.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: replace requests with urllib for Telegram (eventlet-safe)

requests library hangs in daemon threads under gunicorn+eventlet.
Switched to urllib.request which uses stdlib sockets that eventlet
properly monkey-patches. Added _tg_http() helper for both send/poll.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(stdlib): add std/io for stdin/stdout/stderr operations (#202)

* feat(stdlib): add std/io for stdin/stdout/stderr operations

Add std/io module with four functions:
- read_line() -> String: read a line from stdin
- write(s: String): write to stdout (no newline)
- write_err(s: String): write to stderr
- read_all() -> String: read all stdin

Runtime implementation in hew-runtime/src/stdio.rs uses Rust std::io.
Includes E2E test for write/write_err operations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: merge main and apply formatting

Merge main to pick up labelled loop changes. Apply cargo fmt to fix
module ordering in hew-runtime/src/lib.rs.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std::fmt module for number formatting utilities (#207)

Implement pure-Hew number formatting and string padding in std/fmt:

- to_hex(n) — integer to lowercase hexadecimal string
- to_binary(n) — integer to binary string
- to_octal(n) — integer to octal string
- pad_left(s, width, fill) — left-pad string to width
- pad_right(s, width, fill) — right-pad string to width
- repeat(s, count) — repeat string N times

All functions handle negative numbers (prefixed with "-") and zero.
Includes E2E test covering all functions with edge cases.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: hew
repository.description: A statically-typed, actor-oriented programming language for concurrent and distributed systems.

commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std::collections::hashset module (#203)

Expose the existing HashSet runtime FFI as a stdlib module.

The hew-runtime already exports hew_hashset_* functions via #[no_mangle]
extern "C" symbols. This commit adds the Hew-side API that wraps those
FFI bindings with an opaque HashSet handle type, trait methods, and a
module-level constructor.

API: new, insert_int, insert_string, contains_int, contains_string,
remove_int, remove_string, len, is_empty, clear, free.

Includes E2E test covering all operations with both integer and string
element types.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.name: hew
repository.description: A statically-typed, actor-oriented programming language for concurrent and distributed systems.

commit.message:
feat(stdlib): add std::testing assertion library (#201)

Add a pure Hew testing assertion library with the following functions:

- assert_eq(actual, expected) — integer equality
- assert_eq_str(actual, expected) — string equality
- assert_true(condition) — panic if false
- assert_false(condition) — panic if true
- assert_ne(a, b) — panic if equal
- assert_gt(a, b) — panic if a <= b
- assert_lt(a, b) — panic if a >= b

All assertions panic with descriptive messages on failure. No Rust FFI
needed — this is pure Hew.

Includes an E2E test exercising every assertion function.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: min-instances=1 to keep Telegram bot alive, handle 409 conflicts

Cloud Run scales to zero based on HTTP traffic, killing the background
Telegram poll thread. min-instances=1 keeps the container always running.
Added 409 backoff for graceful handling during deploys.

Clear stale optimization metadata when establishing new baseline

Get-PerformanceBaseline.ps1 now deletes experiment-log.md,
experiment-queue.json/md, root-causes/ directory, and hone.jsonl
after database reset (Step 1.6). This prevents:
- Duplicate experiment numbers in the log across runs
- Stale queue items from prior runs being picked up
- Outdated root-cause docs being fed to the fix agent

only one connection can poll a Telegram bot at a time.
